judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations; Report 1 is marginally stronger because it explicitly notes that separate checkouts alone would still collide on the shared results database, while Report 2 compensates with sharper per-engineer setup detail — overall comparable.

report_1.evidence: *Batch B — registry:* F-02, F-05, F-09, all three of which are the entirety of `pipeline/registry.py`. One engineer owns this file; splitting three one-line config fixes across people guarantees conflicts for no gain.

report_2.evidence: **Engineer C — registry config (`pipeline/registry.py`):** F-02, F-05, F-09. All three are edits to one 12-line config file; trivially one person.

report_1.evidence: **Wave 1 (fully parallel, file-disjoint):** *Batch A — tests:* F-11 (rubric edge-case parsing test) and F-12 (pagination boundary test), in `tests/`. ... *Batch B — registry:* F-02, F-05, F-09, all three of which are the entirety of `pipeline/registry.py`. One engineer owns this file ... *Batch C — prompts:* F-03 (`prompts/refusal.txt`) and F-10 (`prompts/grading_system.txt`). Text-only, touches nothing else.

report_2.evidence: Ordering: **A, B, C, D can all start immediately in parallel** — their file sets are disjoint. ... **Engineer B — rubric parsing (`pipeline/rubric.py`):** F-01 plus its test F-11. ... **Engineer C — registry config (`pipeline/registry.py`):** F-02, F-05, F-09. All three are edits to one 12-line config file; trivially one person.

report_1.evidence: **F-06** ("tighten partial-credit thresholds so borderline answers score lower"). This changes scoring outcomes for every graded dataset — it's a product/policy decision, not a bug ... Escalate it for a decision on the actual values (ideally with before/after distributions), without blocking the other batches.

report_2.evidence: **F-06 — a policy decision, not a bug fix.** "Thresholds are too lenient; tighten them" gives no target values, and changing `PARTIAL_CREDIT_THRESHOLDS` silently shifts every score, breaking comparability with all previously recorded results. This needs an owner to specify the new thresholds and decide on re-baselining

report_1.evidence: **Shared test infrastructure:** `tests/conftest.py` points every test run at the same `/var/tmp/llm-scoring/results.db`. Parallel test runs from multiple checkouts would collide on that database unless each engineer sets their own `RESULTS_DB`.

report_2.evidence: `tests/conftest.py:4` reads `RESULTS_DB` (default `/var/tmp/llm-scoring/results.db`); each engineer should set `RESULTS_DB` to a private per-engineer path so parallel test runs don't share one database file.
